title: Dicranopteris spissa
taxonomy: Dicranopteris spissa is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Dicranopteris, which is a group of vascular plants. The genus Dicranopteris is further classified within the order Osmundales and the class Polypodiopsida. The family Matoniaceae is a family of ferns within the division Pteridophyta. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Dicranopteris spissa and its related species.

intro: Dicranopteris spissa, also known as the climbing fern or climbing water fern, is a species of fern in the family Gleicheniaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Bioactive constituents of Dicranopteris spissa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
